original: On Jan. 12, detectives in Montgomery County received a video depicting a 65-year-old day-care operator interacting with a 2-year-old boy in the care of the woman and her husband in the home in the 10800 block of West Ridge Avenue in Wheaton. The boy was crying and crying for help while the woman was yelling at him to stop crying. The woman was later identified by detectives as Susan M. Dutton, 55, of the 10800 block of West Ridge Avenue in Wheaton. Dutton was arrested and charged with one felony count of aggravated assault and one felony count of unlawful restraint on a child under the age of 7 years old. She was also arrested and charged with one felony count of aggravated assault on a child under the age of 13 years old and one felony count of unlawful restraint on a child under the age of 13 years old. She was ordered held without bond on the aggravated assault and unlawful restraint charges and was ordered held on the $75,000 bond on the aggravated assault charge. Dutton was ordered to appear in court on March 15 for the unlawful restraint charge.
